authors_text: Shuai Xie,Zunlei Feng,Ying Chen,Songtao Sun,Chao Ma,Mingli Song
title: DEAL: Difficulty-aware Active Learning for Semantic Segmentation
title_zh: DEAL：语义分割的难度感知主动学习
comment: Accepted by ACCV2020
comments: Accepted by ACCV2020
abstract: Active learning aims to address the paucity of labeled data by finding the most informative samples. However, when applying to semantic segmentation, existing methods ignore the segmentation difficulty of different semantic areas, which leads to poor performance on those hard semantic areas such as tiny or slender objects. To deal with this problem, we propose a semantic Difficulty-awarE Active Learning (DEAL) network composed of two branches: the common segmentation branch and the semantic difficulty branch. For the latter branch, with the supervision of segmentation error between the segmentation result and GT, a pixel-wise probability attention module is introduced to learn the semantic difficulty scores for different semantic areas. Finally, two acquisition functions are devised to select the most valuable samples with semantic difficulty. Competitive results on semantic segmentation benchmarks demonstrate that DEAL achieves state-of-the-art active learning performance and improves the performance of the hard semantic areas in particular.
summary: Active learning aims to address the paucity of labeled data by finding the most informative samples. However, when applying to semantic segmentation, existing methods ignore the segmentation difficulty of different semantic areas, which leads to poor performance on those hard semantic areas such as tiny or slender objects. To deal with this problem, we propose a semantic Difficulty-awarE Active Learning (DEAL) network composed of two branches: the common segmentation branch and the semantic difficulty branch. For the latter branch, with the supervision of segmentation error between the segmentation result and GT, a pixel-wise probability attention module is introduced to learn the semantic difficulty scores for different semantic areas. Finally, two acquisition functions are devised to select the most valuable samples with semantic difficulty. Competitive results on semantic segmentation benchmarks demonstrate that DEAL achieves state-of-the-art active learning performance and improves the performance of the hard semantic areas in particular.
abstract_zh: 主动学习旨在通过寻找信息最丰富的样本来解决标记数据的缺乏问题。然而，在应用于语义分割时，现有方法忽略了不同语义区域的分割难度，导致在微小或细长物体等硬语义区域上表现不佳。为了解决这个问题，我们提出了一种语义难度感知主动学习（DEAL）网络，由两个分支组成：公共分割分支和语义难度分支。对于后一个分支，在分割结果和 GT 之间的分割误差的监督下，引入了逐像素概率注意模块来学习不同语义区域的语义难度分数。最后，设计了两个获取函数来选择具有语义难度的最有价值的样本。语义分割基准的竞争结果表明，DEAL 实现了最先进的主动学习性能，尤其提高了硬语义区域的性能。

AI.tldr: 提出一种面向语义分割的难度感知主动学习框架DEAL，解决现有方法在困难区域（如小物体、细长物体）性能不足的问题。
AI.motivation: 现有主动学习方法在语义分割中未考虑不同语义区域的分割难度差异，导致对小物体、细长等困难区域的分割效果不佳。
AI.method: 设计包含通用分割分支和语义难度分支的DEAL网络，通过分割误差监督引入像素级概率注意力模块计算区域难度分数，并提出两种获取函数选择高价值样本。
AI.result: 在语义分割基准测试中达到最先进水平，尤其显著提升小物体、细长物体等困难区域的分割性能。
AI.conclusion: 通过建模语义区域难度分布并优化样本选择策略，DEAL有效提升了主动学习在语义分割任务中的整体性能，特别是在困难区域。

title: A Systematic Approach to Computing the Manipulator Jacobian and Hessian using the Elementary Transform Sequence
title_zh: 使用初等变换序列计算操纵器雅可比矩阵和海森矩阵的系统方法
abstract: The elementary transform sequence (ETS) provides a universal method of describing the kinematics of any serial-link manipulator. The ETS notation is intuitive and easy to understand, while avoiding the complexity and limitations of Denvit-Hartenberg frame assignment. In this paper, we describe a systematic method for computing the manipulator Jacobian and Hessian (differential kinematics) using the ETS notation. Differential kinematics have many applications including numerical inverse kinematics, resolved-rate motion control and manipulability motion control. Furthermore, we provide an open-source Python library which implements our algorithm and can be interfaced with any serial-link manipulator (available at github.com/petercorke/robotics-toolbox-python).
summary: The elementary transform sequence (ETS) provides a universal method of describing the kinematics of any serial-link manipulator. The ETS notation is intuitive and easy to understand, while avoiding the complexity and limitations of Denvit-Hartenberg frame assignment. In this paper, we describe a systematic method for computing the manipulator Jacobian and Hessian (differential kinematics) using the ETS notation. Differential kinematics have many applications including numerical inverse kinematics, resolved-rate motion control and manipulability motion control. Furthermore, we provide an open-source Python library which implements our algorithm and can be interfaced with any serial-link manipulator (available at github.com/petercorke/robotics-toolbox-python).
abstract_zh: 基本变换序列 (ETS) 提供了描述任何串行链路操纵器运动学的通用方法。 ETS 表示法直观且易于理解，同时避免了 Denvit-Hartenberg 框架分配的复杂性和限制。在本文中，我们描述了一种使用 ETS 符号计算机械臂雅可比行列式和海森行列式（微分运动学）的系统方法。微分运动学有许多应用，包括数值逆运动学、解析速率运动控制和可操纵性运动控制。此外，我们提供了一个开源 Python 库，它实现了我们的算法，并且可以与任何串行链路操纵器连接（可在 github.com/petercorke/robotics-toolbox-python 上获取）。

AI.motivation: 克服传统Denavit-Hartenberg参数法的复杂性和局限性，提供更直观且通用的运动学描述工具。
AI.method: 基于ETS表示机械臂运动学，推导出计算雅可比和海森矩阵的系统方法，并开发开源Python库实现。
AI.result: 实现算法并开发开源Python库，支持任何串联连杆机械臂的接口。
AI.conclusion: ETS方法有效且直观，开源库促进应用，适用于运动控制等场景。

search_text: A Systematic Approach to Computing the Manipulator Jacobian and Hessian using the Elementary Transform Sequence 使用初等变换序列计算操纵器雅可比矩阵和海森矩阵的系统方法 The elementary transform sequence (ETS) provides a universal method of describing the kinematics of any serial-link manipulator. The ETS notation is intuitive and easy to understand, while avoiding the complexity and limitations of Denvit-Hartenberg frame assignment. In this paper, we describe a systematic method for computing the manipulator Jacobian and Hessian (differential kinematics) using the ETS notation. Differential kinematics have many applications including numerical inverse kinematics, resolved-rate motion control and manipulability motion control. Furthermore, we provide an open-source Python library which implements our algorithm and can be interfaced with any serial-link manipulator (available at github.com/petercorke/robotics-toolbox-python). 基本变换序列 (ETS) 提供了描述任何串行链路操纵器运动学的通用方法。 ETS 表示法直观且易于理解，同时避免了 Denvit-Hartenberg 框架分配的复杂性和限制。在本文中，我们描述了一种使用 ETS 符号计算机械臂雅可比行列式和海森行列式（微分运动学）的系统方法。微分运动学有许多应用，包括数值逆运动学、解析速率运动控制和可操纵性运动控制。此外，我们提供了一个开源 Python 库，它实现了我们的算法，并且可以与任何串行链路操纵器连接（可在 github.com/petercorke/robotics-toolbox-python 上获取）。 提出一种基于基本变换序列（ETS）的系统方法，用于计算机械臂的雅可比矩阵和海森矩阵。 Jesse Haviland,Peter Corke cs.RO

authors_text: Kayla Makela,Tim Ophelders,Michelle Quigley,Elizabeth Munch,Daniel Chitwood,Asia Dowtin
title: Automatic Tree Ring Detection using Jacobi Sets
title_zh: 使用雅可比集自动树木年轮检测
abstract: Tree ring widths are an important source of climatic and historical data, but measuring these widths typically requires extensive manual work. Computer vision techniques provide promising directions towards the automation of tree ring detection, but most automated methods still require a substantial amount of user interaction to obtain high accuracy. We perform analysis on 3D X-ray CT images of a cross-section of a tree trunk, known as a tree disk. We present novel automated methods for locating the pith (center) of a tree disk, and ring boundaries. Our methods use a combination of standard image processing techniques and tools from topological data analysis. We evaluate the efficacy of our method for two different CT scans by comparing its results to manually located rings and centers and show that it is better than current automatic methods in terms of correctly counting each ring and its location. Our methods have several parameters, which we optimize experimentally by minimizing edit distances to the manually obtained locations.
summary: Tree ring widths are an important source of climatic and historical data, but measuring these widths typically requires extensive manual work. Computer vision techniques provide promising directions towards the automation of tree ring detection, but most automated methods still require a substantial amount of user interaction to obtain high accuracy. We perform analysis on 3D X-ray CT images of a cross-section of a tree trunk, known as a tree disk. We present novel automated methods for locating the pith (center) of a tree disk, and ring boundaries. Our methods use a combination of standard image processing techniques and tools from topological data analysis. We evaluate the efficacy of our method for two different CT scans by comparing its results to manually located rings and centers and show that it is better than current automatic methods in terms of correctly counting each ring and its location. Our methods have several parameters, which we optimize experimentally by minimizing edit distances to the manually obtained locations.
abstract_zh: 树木年轮宽度是气候和历史数据的重要来源，但测量这些宽度通常需要大量的手工工作。计算机视觉技术为树木年轮检测的自动化提供了有希望的方向，但大多数自动化方法仍然需要大量的用户交互才能获得高精度。我们对树干（称为树盘）横截面的 3D X 射线 CT 图像进行分析。我们提出了用于定位树盘的髓（中心）和环边界的新颖的自动化方法。我们的方法结合使用标准图像处理技术和拓扑数据分析工具。我们通过将结果与手动定位的环和中心进行比较来评估我们的方法对两种不同 CT 扫描的功效，并表明在正确计算每个环及其位置方面，它比当前的自动方法更好。我们的方法有几个参数，我们通过最小化到手动获取位置的编辑距离来进行实验优化。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种基于3D X射线CT图像和拓扑数据分析的自动年轮检测方法，用于定位树盘中心及年轮边界。
AI.motivation: 传统年轮测量依赖人工操作耗时费力，现有自动化方法仍需大量用户交互，需提升检测自动化程度与准确性。
AI.method: 结合标准图像处理技术与拓扑数据分析工具（如Jacobi集），通过实验优化参数以最小化与人工标注结果的编辑距离。
AI.result: 在两种CT扫描数据上验证，方法在年轮计数与定位准确率上优于现有自动方法，接近人工标注水平。
AI.conclusion: 该方法通过参数优化显著提升自动检测性能，为年轮分析提供高效可靠的新方案，具有实际应用价值。

title: Optoelectronic Intelligence
title_zh: 光电智能
comment: 10 pages, five figures, perspective article
comments: 10 pages, five figures, perspective article
abstract: To design and construct hardware for general intelligence, we must consider principles of both neuroscience and very-large-scale integration. For large neural systems capable of general intelligence, the attributes of photonics for communication and electronics for computation are complementary and interdependent. Using light for communication enables high fan-out as well as low-latency signaling across large systems with no traffic-dependent bottlenecks. For computation, the inherent nonlinearities, high speed, and low power consumption of Josephson circuits are conducive to complex neural functions. Operation at 4\,K enables the use of single-photon detectors and silicon light sources, two features that lead to efficiency and economical scalability. Here I sketch a concept for optoelectronic hardware, beginning with synaptic circuits, continuing through wafer-scale integration, and extending to systems interconnected with fiber-optic white matter, potentially at the scale of the human brain and beyond.
summary: To design and construct hardware for general intelligence, we must consider principles of both neuroscience and very-large-scale integration. For large neural systems capable of general intelligence, the attributes of photonics for communication and electronics for computation are complementary and interdependent. Using light for communication enables high fan-out as well as low-latency signaling across large systems with no traffic-dependent bottlenecks. For computation, the inherent nonlinearities, high speed, and low power consumption of Josephson circuits are conducive to complex neural functions. Operation at 4\,K enables the use of single-photon detectors and silicon light sources, two features that lead to efficiency and economical scalability. Here I sketch a concept for optoelectronic hardware, beginning with synaptic circuits, continuing through wafer-scale integration, and extending to systems interconnected with fiber-optic white matter, potentially at the scale of the human brain and beyond.
abstract_zh: 为了设计和构建通用智能的硬件，我们必须考虑神经科学和超大规模集成的原理。对于具有一般智能的大型神经系统，用于通信的光子学和用于计算的电子学的属性是互补且相互依赖的。使用光进行通信可以在大型系统中实现高扇出和低延迟信号传输，并且不存在依赖于流量的瓶颈。对于计算而言，约瑟夫森电路固有的非线性、高速和低功耗有利于复杂的神经功能。在 4\,K 下运行可以使用单光子探测器和硅光源，这两个功能可提高效率和经济的可扩展性。在这里，我概述了光电硬件的概念，从突触电路开始，继续进行晶圆级集成，并扩展到与光纤白质互连的系统，可能达到人脑甚至更远的规模。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种结合光子通信与电子计算的光电子硬件架构，旨在构建类人脑规模的通用智能系统。
AI.motivation: 传统电子硬件在大规模神经网络中存在通信瓶颈与计算效率限制，需融合光子学与超导电子学优势以实现高效智能硬件。
AI.method: 基于神经科学原理与VLSI技术，设计光子通信（低延迟高扇出）与约瑟夫森超导电路计算（非线性高并行）的异构集成方案，采用4K低温实现单光子探测与硅光源的高效协同。
AI.result: 构建了从突触电路到晶圆级集成，再到光纤互联的光电子硬件概念框架，理论上可扩展至人脑规模神经网络。
AI.conclusion: 光电子融合架构通过通信-计算协同优化，为突破传统冯·诺依曼瓶颈、实现类脑通用智能硬件提供了可行技术路径。

Here I sketch a concept for optoelectronic hardware, beginning with synaptic circuits, continuing through wafer-scale integration, and extending to systems interconnected with fiber-optic white matter, potentially at the scale of the human brain and beyond. 为了设计和构建通用智能的硬件，我们必须考虑神经科学和超大规模集成的原理。对于具有一般智能的大型神经系统，用于通信的光子学和用于计算的电子学的属性是互补且相互依赖的。使用光进行通信可以在大型系统中实现高扇出和低延迟信号传输，并且不存在依赖于流量的瓶颈。对于计算而言，约瑟夫森电路固有的非线性、高速和低功耗有利于复杂的神经功能。在 4\,K 下运行可以使用单光子探测器和硅光源，这两个功能可提高效率和经济的可扩展性。在这里，我概述了光电硬件的概念，从突触电路开始，继续进行晶圆级集成，并扩展到与光纤白质互连的系统，可能达到人脑甚至更远的规模。 本文提出了一种结合光子通信与电子计算的光电子硬件架构，旨在构建类人脑规模的通用智能系统。 Jeffrey M. title: NEO: A Novel Expeditious Optimisation Algorithm for Reactive Motion Control of Manipulators
title_zh: NEO：一种用于机械臂反应运动控制的新型快速优化算法
comment: IEEE Robotics and Automation Letters (RA-L). Preprint Version. Accepted January, 2021. The code and videos can be found at https://jhavl.github.io/neo/
comments: IEEE Robotics and Automation Letters (RA-L). Preprint Version. Accepted January, 2021. The code and videos can be found at https://jhavl.github.io/neo/
abstract: We present NEO, a fast and purely reactive motion controller for manipulators which can avoid static and dynamic obstacles while moving to the desired end-effector pose. Additionally, our controller maximises the manipulability of the robot during the trajectory, while avoiding joint position and velocity limits. NEO is wrapped into a strictly convex quadratic programme which, when considering obstacles, joint limits, and manipulability on a 7 degree-of-freedom robot, is generally solved in a few ms. While NEO is not intended to replace state-of-the-art motion planners, our experiments show that it is a viable alternative for scenes with moderate complexity while also being capable of reactive control. For more complex scenes, NEO is better suited as a reactive local controller, in conjunction with a global motion planner. We compare NEO to motion planners on a standard benchmark in simulation and additionally illustrate and verify its operation on a physical robot in a dynamic environment. We provide an open-source library which implements our controller.
summary: We present NEO, a fast and purely reactive motion controller for manipulators which can avoid static and dynamic obstacles while moving to the desired end-effector pose. Additionally, our controller maximises the manipulability of the robot during the trajectory, while avoiding joint position and velocity limits. NEO is wrapped into a strictly convex quadratic programme which, when considering obstacles, joint limits, and manipulability on a 7 degree-of-freedom robot, is generally solved in a few ms. While NEO is not intended to replace state-of-the-art motion planners, our experiments show that it is a viable alternative for scenes with moderate complexity while also being capable of reactive control. For more complex scenes, NEO is better suited as a reactive local controller, in conjunction with a global motion planner. We compare NEO to motion planners on a standard benchmark in simulation and additionally illustrate and verify its operation on a physical robot in a dynamic environment. We provide an open-source library which implements our controller.
abstract_zh: 我们推出 NEO，一种用于机械手的快速、纯反应式运动控制器，它可以避开静态和动态障碍物，同时移动到所需的末端执行器姿势。此外，我们的控制器最大限度地提高了机器人在轨迹过程中的可操作性，同时避免了关节位置和速度限制。 NEO 被包装成严格的凸二次程序，当考虑 7 自由度机器人的障碍物、关节限制和可操作性时，通常可以在几毫秒内解决。虽然 NEO 无意取代最先进的运动规划器，但我们的实验表明，它是中等复杂度场景的可行替代方案，同时还能够进行反应控制。对于更复杂的场景，NEO 更适合作为反应式本地控制器，与全局运动规划器结合使用。我们在模拟中的标准基准上将 NEO 与运动规划器进行比较，并另外说明和验证其在动态环境中的物理机器人上的操作。我们提供了一个实现我们的控制器的开源库。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种名为NEO的新颖快速优化算法，用于机械臂的反应式运动控制，能够避免静态和动态障碍物，并在轨迹中最大化机械臂的可操作性。
AI.motivation: 现有的运动规划算法在复杂环境中响应速度较慢，无法满足实时反应式控制的需求，因此需要一种快速且高效的优化算法。
AI.method: 本文提出了一种基于严格凸二次规划的NEO算法，能够在考虑障碍物、关节限制和可操作性的前提下，快速求解机械臂的运动轨迹。
AI.result: 实验表明，NEO算法能够在几毫秒内求解问题，并在模拟和物理机器人实验中验证了其有效性，适用于中等复杂度场景和作为全局运动规划器的局部控制器。
AI.conclusion: NEO算法作为一种快速反应式控制器，适用于中等复杂度场景，并能作为全局运动规划器的补充，具有良好的应用前景。

title: Example-Driven Intent Prediction with Observers
title_zh: 使用观察者进行示例驱动的意图预测
comment: NAACL 2021
comments: NAACL 2021
abstract: A key challenge of dialog systems research is to effectively and efficiently adapt to new domains. A scalable paradigm for adaptation necessitates the development of generalizable models that perform well in few-shot settings. In this paper, we focus on the intent classification problem which aims to identify user intents given utterances addressed to the dialog system. We propose two approaches for improving the generalizability of utterance classification models: (1) observers and (2) example-driven training. Prior work has shown that BERT-like models tend to attribute a significant amount of attention to the [CLS] token, which we hypothesize results in diluted representations. Observers are tokens that are not attended to, and are an alternative to the [CLS] token as a semantic representation of utterances. Example-driven training learns to classify utterances by comparing to examples, thereby using the underlying encoder as a sentence similarity model. These methods are complementary; improving the representation through observers allows the example-driven model to better measure sentence similarities. When combined, the proposed methods attain state-of-the-art results on three intent prediction datasets (\textsc{banking77}, \textsc{clinc150}, \textsc{hwu64}) in both the full data and few-shot (10 examples per intent) settings. Furthermore, we demonstrate that the proposed approach can transfer to new intents and across datasets without any additional training.
summary: A key challenge of dialog systems research is to effectively and efficiently adapt to new domains. A scalable paradigm for adaptation necessitates the development of generalizable models that perform well in few-shot settings. In this paper, we focus on the intent classification problem which aims to identify user intents given utterances addressed to the dialog system. We propose two approaches for improving the generalizability of utterance classification models: (1) observers and (2) example-driven training. Prior work has shown that BERT-like models tend to attribute a significant amount of attention to the [CLS] token, which we hypothesize results in diluted representations. Observers are tokens that are not attended to, and are an alternative to the [CLS] token as a semantic representation of utterances. Example-driven training learns to classify utterances by comparing to examples, thereby using the underlying encoder as a sentence similarity model. These methods are complementary; improving the representation through observers allows the example-driven model to better measure sentence similarities. When combined, the proposed methods attain state-of-the-art results on three intent prediction datasets (\textsc{banking77}, \textsc{clinc150}, \textsc{hwu64}) in both the full data and few-shot (10 examples per intent) settings. Furthermore, we demonstrate that the proposed approach can transfer to new intents and across datasets without any additional training.
abstract_zh: 对话系统研究的一个关键挑战是有效且高效地适应新领域。可扩展的适应范式需要开发在少数镜头设置中表现良好的通用模型。在本文中，我们重点关注意图分类问题，该问题旨在识别针对对话系统的给定话语的用户意图。我们提出了两种提高话语分类模型的通用性的方法：（1）观察者和（2）示例驱动的训练。之前的工作表明，类似 BERT 的模型倾向于将大量注意力归因于 [CLS] 标记，我们假设这会导致稀释表示。观察者是不被关注的标记，并且是 [CLS] 标记的替代品，作为话语的语义表示。示例驱动的训练通过与示例进行比较来学习对话语进行分类，从而使用底层编码器作为句子相似性模型。这些方法是互补的；通过观察者改进表示使得示例驱动模型能够更好地测量句子相似性。结合起来，所提出的方法在完整数据和少量样本（每个意图 10 个示例）设置中在三个意图预测数据集（\textsc{banking77}、\textsc{clinc150}、\textsc{hwu64}）上获得了最先进的结果。此外，我们证明所提出的方法可以转移到新的意图并跨数据集，而无需任何额外的训练。

AI.tldr: 本文提出两种方法提升对话系统中意图分类模型的泛化能力，解决新领域适应问题。
AI.motivation: 现有模型在少样本场景下表现不佳，且BERT类模型过度依赖[CLS]标记导致表征稀释，需开发更通用的适应方法。
AI.method: （1）引入非注意力标记'观察者'作为替代[CLS]的语义表征；（2）通过示例驱动训练，利用编码器进行句间相似度比较。
AI.result: 在三个意图预测数据集上达到SOTA结果，支持全数据和少样本（每意图10例）设置，并能跨数据集迁移无需额外训练。
AI.conclusion: 结合观察者与示例驱动训练可有效提升意图分类模型的泛化能力与跨领域适应性。

authors_text: Rakesh Shrestha,Zhiwen Fan,Qingkun Su,Zuozhuo Dai,Siyu Zhu,Ping Tan
title: MeshMVS: Multi-View Stereo Guided Mesh Reconstruction
title_zh: MeshMVS：多视图立体引导网格重建
abstract: Deep learning based 3D shape generation methods generally utilize latent features extracted from color images to encode the semantics of objects and guide the shape generation process. These color image semantics only implicitly encode 3D information, potentially limiting the accuracy of the generated shapes. In this paper we propose a multi-view mesh generation method which incorporates geometry information explicitly by using the features from intermediate depth representations of multi-view stereo and regularizing the 3D shapes against these depth images. First, our system predicts a coarse 3D volume from the color images by probabilistically merging voxel occupancy grids from the prediction of individual views. Then the depth images from multi-view stereo along with the rendered depth images of the coarse shape are used as a contrastive input whose features guide the refinement of the coarse shape through a series of graph convolution networks. Notably, we achieve superior results than state-of-the-art multi-view shape generation methods with 34% decrease in Chamfer distance to ground truth and 14% increase in F1-score on ShapeNet dataset.Our source code is available at https://git.io/Jmalg
summary: Deep learning based 3D shape generation methods generally utilize latent features extracted from color images to encode the semantics of objects and guide the shape generation process. These color image semantics only implicitly encode 3D information, potentially limiting the accuracy of the generated shapes. In this paper we propose a multi-view mesh generation method which incorporates geometry information explicitly by using the features from intermediate depth representations of multi-view stereo and regularizing the 3D shapes against these depth images. First, our system predicts a coarse 3D volume from the color images by probabilistically merging voxel occupancy grids from the prediction of individual views. Then the depth images from multi-view stereo along with the rendered depth images of the coarse shape are used as a contrastive input whose features guide the refinement of the coarse shape through a series of graph convolution networks. Notably, we achieve superior results than state-of-the-art multi-view shape generation methods with 34% decrease in Chamfer distance to ground truth and 14% increase in F1-score on ShapeNet dataset.Our source code is available at https://git.io/Jmalg
abstract_zh: 基于深度学习的 3D 形状生成方法通常利用从彩色图像中提取的潜在特征来编码对象的语义并指导形状生成过程。这些彩色图像语义仅隐式编码 3D 信息，可能限制生成形状的准确性。在本文中，我们提出了一种多视图网格生成方法，该方法通过使用多视图立体的中间深度表示的特征并根据这些深度图像正则化 3D 形状来显式地合并几何信息。首先，我们的系统通过概率性地合并来自各个视图预测的体素占用网格，从彩色图像中预测粗略的 3D 体积。然后，来自多视图立体的深度图像以及粗略形状的渲染深度图像被用作对比输入，其特征通过一系列图卷积网络指导粗略形状的细化。值得注意的是，我们取得了比最先进的多视图形状生成方法更好的结果，与地面实况的倒角距离减少了 34%，ShapeNet 数据集上的 F1 分数增加了 14%。我们的源代码可在 https://git.io/Jmalg 上获取。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于多视角立体视觉引导的网格重建方法MeshMVS，通过显式整合几何信息提升3D形状生成精度。
AI.motivation: 现有基于深度学习的3D形状生成方法依赖颜色图像的隐式3D信息编码，导致生成形状精度受限。
AI.method: 首先通过概率合并多视角体素占用网格生成粗略3D体积，再利用多视角立体视觉深度图像与粗略形状渲染深度图像的对比特征，通过图卷积网络迭代优化网格结构。
AI.result: 在ShapeNet数据集上，Chamfer距离较基线下降34%，F1分数提升14%，优于现有最先进的多视角形状生成方法。
AI.conclusion: 通过显式整合多视角立体视觉的几何信息，MeshMVS有效提升了3D网格重建的精度与鲁棒性。

search_text: MeshMVS: Multi-View Stereo Guided Mesh Reconstruction MeshMVS：多视图立体引导网格重建 Deep learning based 3D shape generation methods generally utilize latent features extracted from color images to encode the semantics of objects and guide the shape generation process. These color image semantics only implicitly encode 3D information, potentially limiting the accuracy of the generated shapes. In this paper we propose a multi-view mesh generation method which incorporates geometry information explicitly by using the features from intermediate depth representations of multi-view stereo and regularizing the 3D shapes against these depth images. First, our system predicts a coarse 3D volume from the color images by probabilistically merging voxel occupancy grids from the prediction of individual views. Then the depth images from multi-view stereo along with the rendered depth images of the coarse shape are used as a contrastive input whose features guide the refinement of the coarse shape through a series of graph convolution networks. Notably, we achieve superior results than state-of-the-art multi-view shape generation methods with 34% decrease in Chamfer distance to ground truth and 14% increase in F1-score on ShapeNet dataset.Our source code is available at https://git.io/Jmalg 基于深度学习的 3D 形状生成方法通常利用从彩色图像中提取的潜在特征来编码对象的语义并指导形状生成过程。这些彩色图像语义仅隐式编码 3D 信息，可能限制生成形状的准确性。在本文中，我们提出了一种多视图网格生成方法，该方法通过使用多视图立体的中间深度表示的特征并根据这些深度图像正则化 3D 形状来显式地合并几何信息。首先，我们的系统通过概率性地合并来自各个视图预测的体素占用网格，从彩色图像中预测粗略的 3D 体积。然后，来自多视图立体的深度图像以及粗略形状的渲染深度图像被用作对比输入，其特征通过一系列图卷积网络指导粗略形状的细化。值得注意的是，我们取得了比最先进的多视图形状生成方法更好的结果，与地面实况的倒角距离减少了 34%，ShapeNet 数据集上的 F1 分数增加了 14%。我们的源代码可在 https://git.io/Jmalg 上获取。 本文提出一种基于多视角立体视觉引导的网格重建方法MeshMVS，通过显式整合几何信息提升3D形状生成精度。 Rakesh Shrestha,Zhiwen Fan,Qingkun Su,Zuozhuo Dai,Siyu Zhu,Ping Tan cs.CV,cs.LG,eess.IV

authors_text: Robert David,Jared Duke,Advait Jain,Vijay Janapa Reddi,Nat Jeffries,Jian Li,Nick Kreeger,Ian Nappier,Meghna Natraj,Shlomi Regev,Rocky Rhodes,Tiezhen Wang,Pete Warden
title: TensorFlow Lite Micro: Embedded Machine Learning on TinyML Systems
title_zh: TensorFlow Lite Micro：TinyML 系统上的嵌入式机器学习
abstract: Deep learning inference on embedded devices is a burgeoning field with myriad applications because tiny embedded devices are omnipresent. But we must overcome major challenges before we can benefit from this opportunity. Embedded processors are severely resource constrained. Their nearest mobile counterparts exhibit at least a 100 -- 1,000x difference in compute capability, memory availability, and power consumption. As a result, the machine-learning (ML) models and associated ML inference framework must not only execute efficiently but also operate in a few kilobytes of memory. Also, the embedded devices' ecosystem is heavily fragmented. To maximize efficiency, system vendors often omit many features that commonly appear in mainstream systems, including dynamic memory allocation and virtual memory, that allow for cross-platform interoperability. The hardware comes in many flavors (e.g., instruction-set architecture and FPU support, or lack thereof). We introduce TensorFlow Lite Micro (TF Micro), an open-source ML inference framework for running deep-learning models on embedded systems. TF Micro tackles the efficiency requirements imposed by embedded-system resource constraints and the fragmentation challenges that make cross-platform interoperability nearly impossible. The framework adopts a unique interpreter-based approach that provides flexibility while overcoming these challenges. This paper explains the design decisions behind TF Micro and describes its implementation details. Also, we present an evaluation to demonstrate its low resource requirement and minimal run-time performance overhead.
summary: Deep learning inference on embedded devices is a burgeoning field with myriad applications because tiny embedded devices are omnipresent. But we must overcome major challenges before we can benefit from this opportunity. Embedded processors are severely resource constrained. Their nearest mobile counterparts exhibit at least a 100 -- 1,000x difference in compute capability, memory availability, and power consumption. As a result, the machine-learning (ML) models and associated ML inference framework must not only execute efficiently but also operate in a few kilobytes of memory. Also, the embedded devices' ecosystem is heavily fragmented. To maximize efficiency, system vendors often omit many features that commonly appear in mainstream systems, including dynamic memory allocation and virtual memory, that allow for cross-platform interoperability. The hardware comes in many flavors (e.g., instruction-set architecture and FPU support, or lack thereof). We introduce TensorFlow Lite Micro (TF Micro), an open-source ML inference framework for running deep-learning models on embedded systems. TF Micro tackles the efficiency requirements imposed by embedded-system resource constraints and the fragmentation challenges that make cross-platform interoperability nearly impossible. The framework adopts a unique interpreter-based approach that provides flexibility while overcoming these challenges. This paper explains the design decisions behind TF Micro and describes its implementation details. Also, we present an evaluation to demonstrate its low resource requirement and minimal run-time performance overhead.
abstract_zh: 嵌入式设备上的深度学习推理是一个新兴领域，有着无数的应用，因为微型嵌入式设备无处不在。但我们必须克服重大挑战才能从这个机会中受益。嵌入式处理器的资源受到严重限制。它们最接近的移动同类产品在计算能力、内存可用性和功耗方面至少存在 100 至 1,000 倍的差异。因此，机器学习 (ML) 模型和相关的 ML 推理框架不仅必须高效执行，而且必须在几千字节的内存中运行。此外，嵌入式设备的生态系统严重分散。为了最大限度地提高效率，系统供应商经常忽略主流系统中常见的许多功能，包括动态内存分配和虚拟内存，以实现跨平台互操作性。硬件有多种风格（例如，指令集架构和 FPU 支持，或缺乏）。我们推出 TensorFlow Lite Micro (TF Micro)，这是一种开源机器学习推理框架，用于在嵌入式系统上运行深度学习模型。 TF Micro 解决了嵌入式系统资源限制所带来的效率要求以及使跨平台互操作性几乎不可能的碎片化挑战。该框架采用独特的基于解释器的方法，在克服这些挑战的同时提供灵活性。本文解释了 TF Micro 背后的设计决策并描述了其实现细节。此外，我们还进行了评估，以证明其低资源需求和最小的运行时性能开销。

AI.tldr: 本文介绍了TensorFlow Lite Micro（TF Micro）框架，旨在解决嵌入式设备上深度学习推理的资源限制和跨平台兼容性挑战。
AI.motivation: 嵌入式设备资源极度受限且生态系统碎片化，传统ML框架难以满足其高效运行和跨平台需求。
AI.method: 采用基于解释器的架构，省略动态内存分配等特性，优化模型执行效率并适配多样化硬件环境。
AI.result: 评估表明TF Micro在资源占用和运行时开销方面表现优异，可有效运行于数KB内存的嵌入式系统。
AI.conclusion: TF Micro通过灵活的架构设计和资源优化，为TinyML系统提供了可行的深度学习推理解决方案。

search_text: TensorFlow Lite Micro: Embedded Machine Learning on TinyML Systems TensorFlow Lite Micro：TinyML 系统上的嵌入式机器学习 Deep learning inference on embedded devices is a burgeoning field with myriad applications because tiny embedded devices are omnipresent. But we must overcome major challenges before we can benefit from this opportunity. Embedded processors are severely resource constrained. Their nearest mobile counterparts exhibit at least a 100 -- 1,000x difference in compute capability, memory availability, and power consumption. As a result, the machine-learning (ML) models and associated ML inference framework must not only execute efficiently but also operate in a few kilobytes of memory. Also, the embedded devices' ecosystem is heavily fragmented. To maximize efficiency, system vendors often omit many features that commonly appear in mainstream systems, including dynamic memory allocation and virtual memory, that allow for cross-platform interoperability. The hardware comes in many flavors (e.g., instruction-set architecture and FPU support, or lack thereof). We introduce TensorFlow Lite Micro (TF Micro), an open-source ML inference framework for running deep-learning models on embedded systems. TF Micro tackles the efficiency requirements imposed by embedded-system resource constraints and the fragmentation challenges that make cross-platform interoperability nearly impossible. The framework adopts a unique interpreter-based approach that provides flexibility while overcoming these challenges. This paper explains the design decisions behind TF Micro and describes its implementation details. Also, we present an evaluation to demonstrate its low resource requirement and minimal run-time performance overhead. 嵌入式设备上的深度学习推理是一个新兴领域，有着无数的应用，因为微型嵌入式设备无处不在。但我们必须克服重大挑战才能从这个机会中受益。嵌入式处理器的资源受到严重限制。它们最接近的移动同类产品在计算能力、内存可用性和功耗方面至少存在 100 至 1,000 倍的差异。因此，机器学习 (ML) 模型和相关的 ML 推理框架不仅必须高效执行，而且必须在几千字节的内存中运行。此外，嵌入式设备的生态系统严重分散。为了最大限度地提高效率，系统供应商经常忽略主流系统中常见的许多功能，包括动态内存分配和虚拟内存，以实现跨平台互操作性。硬件有多种风格（例如，指令集架构和 FPU 支持，或缺乏）。我们推出 TensorFlow Lite Micro (TF Micro)，这是一种开源机器学习推理框架，用于在嵌入式系统上运行深度学习模型。 TF Micro 解决了嵌入式系统资源限制所带来的效率要求以及使跨平台互操作性几乎不可能的碎片化挑战。该框架采用独特的基于解释器的方法，在克服这些挑战的同时提供灵活性。本文解释了 TF Micro 背后的设计决策并描述了其实现细节。此外，我们还进行了评估，以证明其低资源需求和最小的运行时性能开销。 本文介绍了TensorFlow Lite Micro（TF Micro）框架，旨在解决嵌入式设备上深度学习推理的资源限制和跨平台兼容性挑战。 Robert David,Jared Duke,Advait Jain,Vijay Janapa Reddi,Nat Jeffries,Jian Li,Nick Kreeger,Ian Nappier,Meghna Natraj,Shlomi Regev,Rocky Rhodes,Tiezhen Wang,Pete Warden cs.LG,cs.AI

authors_text: Anar Amgalan,Lilianne R. Mujica-Parodi,Steven S. Skiena
title: Fast Spatial Autocorrelation
title_zh: 快速空间自相关
comment: To be published in ICDM 2020
comments: To be published in ICDM 2020
abstract: Physical or geographic location proves to be an important feature in many data science models, because many diverse natural and social phenomenon have a spatial component. Spatial autocorrelation measures the extent to which locally adjacent observations of the same phenomenon are correlated. Although statistics like Moran's $I$ and Geary's $C$ are widely used to measure spatial autocorrelation, they are slow: all popular methods run in $Ω(n^2)$ time, rendering them unusable for large data sets, or long time-courses with moderate numbers of points. We propose a new $S_A$ statistic based on the notion that the variance observed when merging pairs of nearby clusters should increase slowly for spatially autocorrelated variables. We give a linear-time algorithm to calculate $S_A$ for a variable with an input agglomeration order (available at https://github.com/aamgalan/spatial_autocorrelation). For a typical dataset of $n \approx 63,000$ points, our $S_A$ autocorrelation measure can be computed in 1 second, versus 2 hours or more for Moran's $I$ and Geary's $C$. Through simulation studies, we demonstrate that $S_A$ identifies spatial correlations in variables generated with spatially-dependent model half an order of magnitude earlier than either Moran's $I$ or Geary's $C$. Finally, we prove several theoretical properties of $S_A$: namely that it behaves as a true correlation statistic, and is invariant under addition or multiplication by a constant.
summary: Physical or geographic location proves to be an important feature in many data science models, because many diverse natural and social phenomenon have a spatial component. Spatial autocorrelation measures the extent to which locally adjacent observations of the same phenomenon are correlated. Although statistics like Moran's $I$ and Geary's $C$ are widely used to measure spatial autocorrelation, they are slow: all popular methods run in $Ω(n^2)$ time, rendering them unusable for large data sets, or long time-courses with moderate numbers of points. We propose a new $S_A$ statistic based on the notion that the variance observed when merging pairs of nearby clusters should increase slowly for spatially autocorrelated variables. We give a linear-time algorithm to calculate $S_A$ for a variable with an input agglomeration order (available at https://github.com/aamgalan/spatial_autocorrelation). For a typical dataset of $n \approx 63,000$ points, our $S_A$ autocorrelation measure can be computed in 1 second, versus 2 hours or more for Moran's $I$ and Geary's $C$. Through simulation studies, we demonstrate that $S_A$ identifies spatial correlations in variables generated with spatially-dependent model half an order of magnitude earlier than either Moran's $I$ or Geary's $C$. Finally, we prove several theoretical properties of $S_A$: namely that it behaves as a true correlation statistic, and is invariant under addition or multiplication by a constant.
abstract_zh: 物理或地理位置被证明是许多数据科学模型中的重要特征，因为许多不同的自然和社会现象都具有空间成分。空间自相关测量同一现象的局部相邻观测值的相关程度。尽管 Moran 的 $I$ 和 Geary 的 $C$ 等统计数据被广泛用于测量空间自相关，但它们的速度很慢：所有流行的方法都需要 $Ω(n^2)$ 时间运行，这使得它们无法用于大型数据集或具有中等数量点的长时间过程。我们提出了一种新的 $S_A$ 统计量，其基础是这样的概念：合并附近簇对时观察到的方差对于空间自相关变量应该缓慢增加。我们给出了一个线性时间算法来计算具有输入聚集顺序的变量的 $S_A$ （可在 https://github.com/aamgalan/spatial_autocorrelation 获得）。对于 $n \大约 63,000$ 个点的典型数据集，我们的 $S_A$ 自相关度量可以在 1 秒内计算出来，而 Moran 的 $I$ 和 Geary 的 $C$ 则需要 2 小时或更长时间。通过模拟研究，我们证明 $S_A$ 识别空间相关模型生成的变量的空间相关性比 Moran 的 $I$ 或 Geary 的 $C$ 早半个数量级。最后，我们证明了 $S_A$ 的几个理论属性：即它表现为真正的相关统计量，并且在与常数相加或相乘时保持不变。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种名为$S_A$的快速空间自相关统计量，用于解决传统方法计算效率低的问题。
AI.motivation: 传统空间自相关指标（如Moran's $I$和Geary's $C$）计算复杂度为$Ω(n^2)$，难以处理大规模数据集。
AI.method: 基于聚类合并时方差变化的特性，设计线性时间算法计算$S_A$统计量，并提供公开的聚类顺序输入接口。

search_text: Fast Spatial Autocorrelation 快速空间自相关 Physical or geographic location proves to be an important feature in many data science models, because many diverse natural and social phenomenon have a spatial component. Spatial autocorrelation measures the extent to which locally adjacent observations of the same phenomenon are correlated. Although statistics like Moran's $I$ and Geary's $C$ are widely used to measure spatial autocorrelation, they are slow: all popular methods run in $Ω(n^2)$ time, rendering them unusable for large data sets, or long time-courses with moderate numbers of points. We propose a new $S_A$ statistic based on the notion that the variance observed when merging pairs of nearby clusters should increase slowly for spatially autocorrelated variables. We give a linear-time algorithm to calculate $S_A$ for a variable with an input agglomeration order (available at https://github.com/aamgalan/spatial_autocorrelation). For a typical dataset of $n \approx 63,000$ points, our $S_A$ autocorrelation measure can be computed in 1 second, versus 2 hours or more for Moran's $I$ and Geary's $C$. Through simulation studies, we demonstrate that $S_A$ identifies spatial correlations in variables generated with spatially-dependent model half an order of magnitude earlier than either Moran's $I$ or Geary's $C$. Finally, we prove several theoretical properties of $S_A$: namely that it behaves as a true correlation statistic, and is invariant under addition or multiplication by a constant. 物理或地理位置被证明是许多数据科学模型中的重要特征，因为许多不同的自然和社会现象都具有空间成分。空间自相关测量同一现象的局部相邻观测值的相关程度。尽管 Moran 的 $I$ 和 Geary 的 $C$ 等统计数据被广泛用于测量空间自相关，但它们的速度很慢：所有流行的方法都需要 $Ω(n^2)$ 时间运行，这使得它们无法用于大型数据集或具有中等数量点的长时间过程。我们提出了一种新的 $S_A$ 统计量，其基础是这样的概念：合并附近簇对时观察到的方差对于空间自相关变量应该缓慢增加。我们给出了一个线性时间算法来计算具有输入聚集顺序的变量的 $S_A$ （可在 https://github.com/aamgalan/spatial_autocorrelation 获得）。对于 $n \大约 63,000$ 个点的典型数据集，我们的 $S_A$ 自相关度量可以在 1 秒内计算出来，而 Moran 的 $I$ 和 Geary 的 $C$ 则需要 2 小时或更长时间。通过模拟研究，我们证明 $S_A$ 识别空间相关模型生成的变量的空间相关性比 Moran 的 $I$ 或 Geary 的 $C$ 早半个数量级。最后，我们证明了 $S_A$ 的几个理论属性：即它表现为真正的相关统计量，并且在与常数相加或相乘时保持不变。 本文提出了一种名为$S_A$的快速空间自相关统计量，用于解决传统方法计算效率低的问题。 Anar Amgalan,Lilianne R. Mujica-Parodi,Steven S. Skiena stat.ME,cs.DS

title: Long-Term Face Tracking for Crowded Video-Surveillance Scenarios
title_zh: 拥挤视频监控场景的长期人脸追踪
comment: 8 pages, 6 figures, 4 tables. Published in the International Joint Conference on Biometrics, 2020
comments: 8 pages, 6 figures, 4 tables. Published in the International Joint Conference on Biometrics, 2020
abstract: Most current multi-object trackers focus on short-term tracking, and are based on deep and complex systems that do not operate in real-time, often making them impractical for video-surveillance. In this paper, we present a long-term multi-face tracking architecture conceived for working in crowded contexts, particularly unconstrained in terms of movement and occlusions, and where the face is often the only visible part of the person. Our system benefits from advances in the fields of face detection and face recognition to achieve long-term tracking. It follows a tracking-by-detection approach, combining a fast short-term visual tracker with a novel online tracklet reconnection strategy grounded on face verification. Additionally, a correction module is included to correct past track assignments with no extra computational cost. We present a series of experiments introducing novel, specialized metrics for the evaluation of long-term tracking capabilities and a video dataset that we publicly release. Findings demonstrate that, in this context, our approach allows to obtain up to 50% longer tracks than state-of-the-art deep learning trackers.
summary: Most current multi-object trackers focus on short-term tracking, and are based on deep and complex systems that do not operate in real-time, often making them impractical for video-surveillance. In this paper, we present a long-term multi-face tracking architecture conceived for working in crowded contexts, particularly unconstrained in terms of movement and occlusions, and where the face is often the only visible part of the person. Our system benefits from advances in the fields of face detection and face recognition to achieve long-term tracking. It follows a tracking-by-detection approach, combining a fast short-term visual tracker with a novel online tracklet reconnection strategy grounded on face verification. Additionally, a correction module is included to correct past track assignments with no extra computational cost. We present a series of experiments introducing novel, specialized metrics for the evaluation of long-term tracking capabilities and a video dataset that we publicly release. Findings demonstrate that, in this context, our approach allows to obtain up to 50% longer tracks than state-of-the-art deep learning trackers.
abstract_zh: 目前大多数多目标跟踪器都专注于短期跟踪，并且基于不实时运行的深层且复杂的系统，这通常使得它们对于视频监控来说不切实际。在本文中，我们提出了一种长期多面部跟踪架构，专为在拥挤的环境中工作而设计，特别是在运动和遮挡方面不受限制，并且面部通常是人的唯一可见部分的情况。我们的系统受益于人脸检测和人脸识别领域的进步，以实现长期跟踪。它遵循检测跟踪方法，将快速短期视觉跟踪器与基于人脸验证的新颖在线轨迹重新连接策略相结合。此外，还包含一个校正模块来校正过去的轨道分配，而无需额外的计算成本。我们提出了一系列实验，引入了用于评估长期跟踪能力的新颖、专门的指标以及我们公开发布的视频数据集。研究结果表明，在这种情况下，我们的方法可以获得比最先进的深度学习跟踪器长 50% 的轨迹。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种适用于拥挤视频监控场景的长期多人脸跟踪架构，解决现有跟踪器无法实时运行及应对复杂遮挡的问题。
AI.motivation: 现有深度学习跟踪器多为短期跟踪，难以适应视频监控中人群密集、遮挡频繁且仅能依赖人脸可见的场景需求。
AI.method: 采用跟踪-by-检测框架，结合快速短期视觉跟踪器与基于人脸验证的在线tracklet重连接策略，并引入无额外计算成本的轨迹校正模块。
AI.result: 在自建数据集上实验表明，该方法实现比现有深度学习跟踪器长达50%的轨迹长度，且通过新型评估指标验证了长期跟踪能力。
AI.conclusion: 本文方法通过融合人脸检测与验证技术，有效提升了复杂场景下长期人脸跟踪的鲁棒性与实用性。

authors_text: Alonso Marco,Dominik Baumann,Majid Khadiv,Philipp Hennig,Ludovic Righetti,Sebastian Trimpe
title: Robot Learning with Crash Constraints
title_zh: 具有碰撞约束的机器人学习
comment: 8 pages, 4 figures, 1 table, 1 algorithm. Accepted for publication in IEEE Robotics and Automation Letters (RA-L). Video demonstration of the experiments available at https://youtu.be/RAiIo0l6_rE . Algorithm implementation available at https://github.com/alonrot/classified_regression.git
comments: 8 pages, 4 figures, 1 table, 1 algorithm. Accepted for publication in IEEE Robotics and Automation Letters (RA-L). Video demonstration of the experiments available at https://youtu.be/RAiIo0l6_rE . Algorithm implementation available at https://github.com/alonrot/classified_regression.git
abstract: In the past decade, numerous machine learning algorithms have been shown to successfully learn optimal policies to control real robotic systems. However, it is common to encounter failing behaviors as the learning loop progresses. Specifically, in robot applications where failing is undesired but not catastrophic, many algorithms struggle with leveraging data obtained from failures. This is usually caused by (i) the failed experiment ending prematurely, or (ii) the acquired data being scarce or corrupted. Both complicate the design of proper reward functions to penalize failures. In this paper, we propose a framework that addresses those issues. We consider failing behaviors as those that violate a constraint and address the problem of learning with crash constraints, where no data is obtained upon constraint violation. The no-data case is addressed by a novel GP model (GPCR) for the constraint that combines discrete events (failure/success) with continuous observations (only obtained upon success). We demonstrate the effectiveness of our framework on simulated benchmarks and on a real jumping quadruped, where the constraint threshold is unknown a priori. Experimental data is collected, by means of constrained Bayesian optimization, directly on the real robot. Our results outperform manual tuning and GPCR proves useful on estimating the constraint threshold.
summary: In the past decade, numerous machine learning algorithms have been shown to successfully learn optimal policies to control real robotic systems. However, it is common to encounter failing behaviors as the learning loop progresses. Specifically, in robot applications where failing is undesired but not catastrophic, many algorithms struggle with leveraging data obtained from failures. This is usually caused by (i) the failed experiment ending prematurely, or (ii) the acquired data being scarce or corrupted. Both complicate the design of proper reward functions to penalize failures. In this paper, we propose a framework that addresses those issues. We consider failing behaviors as those that violate a constraint and address the problem of learning with crash constraints, where no data is obtained upon constraint violation. The no-data case is addressed by a novel GP model (GPCR) for the constraint that combines discrete events (failure/success) with continuous observations (only obtained upon success). We demonstrate the effectiveness of our framework on simulated benchmarks and on a real jumping quadruped, where the constraint threshold is unknown a priori. Experimental data is collected, by means of constrained Bayesian optimization, directly on the real robot. Our results outperform manual tuning and GPCR proves useful on estimating the constraint threshold.
abstract_zh: 在过去的十年中，许多机器学习算法已被证明可以成功学习控制真实机器人系统的最佳策略。然而，随着学习循环的进展，经常会遇到失败的行为。具体来说，在机器人应用中，失败是不受欢迎的，但不是灾难性的，许多算法都在努力利用从失败中获得的数据。这通常是由于 (i) 失败的实验提前结束，或 (ii) 获取的数据稀缺或损坏造成的。两者都使惩罚失败的适当奖励函数的设计变得复杂。在本文中，我们提出了一个解决这些问题的框架。我们将失败行为视为违反约束的行为，并解决使用崩溃约束进行学习的问题，其中在违反约束时无法获得任何数据。无数据情况通过新颖的 GP 模型 (GPCR) 解决，该模型将离散事件（失败/成功）与连续观察（仅在成功时获得）相结合。我们在模拟基准和真实的跳跃四足动物上展示了我们的框架的有效性，其中约束阈值是先验未知的。通过约束贝叶斯优化直接在真实机器人上收集实验数据。我们的结果优于手动调整，并且 GPCR 在估计约束阈值方面被证明是有用的。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种处理机器人学习中失败行为的框架，通过引入约束条件并结合高斯过程模型（GPCR）解决无失败数据的学习问题。
AI.motivation: 现有算法难以利用失败数据设计有效的奖励函数，导致机器人学习过程中失败行为无法被有效约束，尤其在失败不产生数据或数据稀缺时问题更突出。
AI.method: 提出GPCR模型，将离散的失败/成功事件与连续的成功观测数据结合，通过约束贝叶斯优化在真实机器人上直接收集数据，无需依赖失败数据。
AI.result: 框架在模拟基准和实际跳跃四足机器人实验中表现优于手动调参，GPCR成功估计了未知的约束阈值。
AI.conclusion: 该方法有效解决了无失败数据的约束学习问题，为机器人安全学习提供了新思路。

ai_content_json.result: 框架在模拟基准和实际跳跃四足机器人实验中表现优于手动调参，GPCR成功估计了未知的约束阈值。
ai_content_json.conclusion: 该方法有效解决了无失败数据的约束学习问题，为机器人安全学习提供了新思路。

tldr: 本文提出了一种处理机器人学习中失败行为的框架，通过引入约束条件并结合高斯过程模型（GPCR）解决无失败数据的学习问题。
motivation: 现有算法难以利用失败数据设计有效的奖励函数，导致机器人学习过程中失败行为无法被有效约束，尤其在失败不产生数据或数据稀缺时问题更突出。

conclusion: 该方法有效解决了无失败数据的约束学习问题，为机器人安全学习提供了新思路。
search_text: Robot Learning with Crash Constraints 具有碰撞约束的机器人学习 In the past decade, numerous machine learning algorithms have been shown to successfully learn optimal policies to control real robotic systems. However, it is common to encounter failing behaviors as the learning loop progresses. Specifically, in robot applications where failing is undesired but not catastrophic, many algorithms struggle with leveraging data obtained from failures. This is usually caused by (i) the failed experiment ending prematurely, or (ii) the acquired data being scarce or corrupted. Both complicate the design of proper reward functions to penalize failures. In this paper, we propose a framework that addresses those issues. We consider failing behaviors as those that violate a constraint and address the problem of learning with crash constraints, where no data is obtained upon constraint violation. The no-data case is addressed by a novel GP model (GPCR) for the constraint that combines discrete events (failure/success) with continuous observations (only obtained upon success). We demonstrate the effectiveness of our framework on simulated benchmarks and on a real jumping quadruped, where the constraint threshold is unknown a priori. Experimental data is collected, by means of constrained Bayesian optimization, directly on the real robot. Our results outperform manual tuning and GPCR proves useful on estimating the constraint threshold. 在过去的十年中，许多机器学习算法已被证明可以成功学习控制真实机器人系统的最佳策略。然而，随着学习循环的进展，经常会遇到失败的行为。具体来说，在机器人应用中，失败是不受欢迎的，但不是灾难性的，许多算法都在努力利用从失败中获得的数据。这通常是由于 (i) 失败的实验提前结束，或 (ii) 获取的数据稀缺或损坏造成的。两者都使惩罚失败的适当奖励函数的设计变得复杂。在本文中，我们提出了一个解决这些问题的框架。我们将失败行为视为违反约束的行为，并解决使用崩溃约束进行学习的问题，其中在违反约束时无法获得任何数据。无数据情况通过新颖的 GP 模型 (GPCR) 解决，该模型将离散事件（失败/成功）与连续观察（仅在成功时获得）相结合。我们在模拟基准和真实的跳跃四足动物上展示了我们的框架的有效性，其中约束阈值是先验未知的。通过约束贝叶斯优化直接在真实机器人上收集实验数据。我们的结果优于手动调整，并且 GPCR 在估计约束阈值方面被证明是有用的。 本文提出了一种处理机器人学习中失败行为的框架，通过引入约束条件并结合高斯过程模型（GPCR）解决无失败数据的学习问题。 Alonso Marco,Dominik Baumann,Majid Khadiv,Philipp Hennig,Ludovic Righetti,Sebastian Trimpe cs.RO,cs.LG

title: Active Domain Adaptation via Clustering Uncertainty-weighted Embeddings
title_zh: 通过聚类不确定性加权嵌入进行主动域适应
comment: Published at ICCV 2021. Our code is available at https://github.com/virajprabhu/CLUE
comments: Published at ICCV 2021. Our code is available at https://github.com/virajprabhu/CLUE
abstract: Generalizing deep neural networks to new target domains is critical to their real-world utility. In practice, it may be feasible to get some target data labeled, but to be cost-effective it is desirable to select a maximally-informative subset via active learning (AL). We study the problem of AL under a domain shift, called Active Domain Adaptation (Active DA). We demonstrate how existing AL approaches based solely on model uncertainty or diversity sampling are less effective for Active DA. We propose Clustering Uncertainty-weighted Embeddings (CLUE), a novel label acquisition strategy for Active DA that performs uncertainty-weighted clustering to identify target instances for labeling that are both uncertain under the model and diverse in feature space. CLUE consistently outperforms competing label acquisition strategies for Active DA and AL across learning settings on 6 diverse domain shifts for image classification.
summary: Generalizing deep neural networks to new target domains is critical to their real-world utility. In practice, it may be feasible to get some target data labeled, but to be cost-effective it is desirable to select a maximally-informative subset via active learning (AL). We study the problem of AL under a domain shift, called Active Domain Adaptation (Active DA). We demonstrate how existing AL approaches based solely on model uncertainty or diversity sampling are less effective for Active DA. We propose Clustering Uncertainty-weighted Embeddings (CLUE), a novel label acquisition strategy for Active DA that performs uncertainty-weighted clustering to identify target instances for labeling that are both uncertain under the model and diverse in feature space. CLUE consistently outperforms competing label acquisition strategies for Active DA and AL across learning settings on 6 diverse domain shifts for image classification.
abstract_zh: 将深度神经网络推广到新的目标领域对其现实世界的实用性至关重要。在实践中，标记一些目标数据可能是可行的，但为了具有成本效益，最好通过主动学习（AL）选择信息量最大的子集。我们研究域转移下的 AL 问题，称为主动域适应（Active DA）。我们证明了现有的仅基于模型不确定性或多样性采样的 AL 方法对于 Active DA 来说效果较差。我们提出了聚类不确定性加权嵌入（CLUE），这是一种针对 Active DA 的新型标签获取策略，它执行不确定性加权聚类来识别模型下不确定且特征空间多样化的目标实例进行标记。在图像分类的 6 个不同域转换的学习设置中，CLUE 始终优于 Active DA 和 AL 的竞争标签获取策略。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种名为CLUE的主动领域自适应方法，通过不确定性加权聚类来优化目标领域的标签选择。
AI.motivation: 现有主动学习方法在领域偏移场景下效果不佳，需设计更有效的标签选择策略以提升模型在目标领域的泛化能力。
AI.method: 提出CLUE方法，结合模型不确定性与特征空间多样性，对目标样本嵌入进行加权聚类，筛选出最具信息量的样本进行标注。
AI.result: CLUE在6个图像分类领域的偏移任务中，均优于现有主动学习及领域自适应方法。
AI.conclusion: CLUE通过不确定性与多样性联合优化，有效提升了主动领域自适应的性能，为标签高效利用提供了新思路。

authors_text: Robin Richter,Duy H. Thai,Stephan F. Huckemann
title: Generalized Intersection Algorithms with Fixpoints for Image Decomposition Learning
title_zh: 用于图像分解学习的带有不动点的广义交集算法
comment: 30 pages, 4 figures
comments: 30 pages, 4 figures
abstract: In image processing, classical methods minimize a suitable functional that balances between computational feasibility (convexity of the functional is ideal) and suitable penalties reflecting the desired image decomposition. The fact that algorithms derived from such minimization problems can be used to construct (deep) learning architectures has spurred the development of algorithms that can be trained for a specifically desired image decomposition, e.g. into cartoon and texture. While many such methods are very successful, theoretical guarantees are only scarcely available. To this end, in this contribution, we formalize a general class of intersection point problems encompassing a wide range of (learned) image decomposition models, and we give an existence result for a large subclass of such problems, i.e. giving the existence of a fixpoint of the corresponding algorithm. This class generalizes classical model-based variational problems, such as the TV-l2 -model or the more general TV-Hilbert model. To illustrate the potential for learned algorithms, novel (non learned) choices within our class show comparable results in denoising and texture removal.
summary: In image processing, classical methods minimize a suitable functional that balances between computational feasibility (convexity of the functional is ideal) and suitable penalties reflecting the desired image decomposition. The fact that algorithms derived from such minimization problems can be used to construct (deep) learning architectures has spurred the development of algorithms that can be trained for a specifically desired image decomposition, e.g. into cartoon and texture. While many such methods are very successful, theoretical guarantees are only scarcely available. To this end, in this contribution, we formalize a general class of intersection point problems encompassing a wide range of (learned) image decomposition models, and we give an existence result for a large subclass of such problems, i.e. giving the existence of a fixpoint of the corresponding algorithm. This class generalizes classical model-based variational problems, such as the TV-l2 -model or the more general TV-Hilbert model. To illustrate the potential for learned algorithms, novel (non learned) choices within our class show comparable results in denoising and texture removal.
abstract_zh: 在图像处理中，经典方法最小化合适的函数，该函数在计算可行性（函数的凸性是理想的）和反映所需图像分解的合适惩罚之间取得平衡。从此类最小化问题导出的算法可用于构建（深度）学习架构，这一事实刺激了可针对特定所需图像分解进行训练的算法的开发，例如卡通和纹理。尽管许多此类方法非常成功，但几乎没有理论保证。为此，在本贡献中，我们形式化了一类包含广泛（学习的）图像分解模型的交点问题，并给出了此类问题的大子类的存在性结果，即给出了相应算法的不动点的存在性。此类概括了经典的基于模型的变分问题，例如 TV-l2 模型或更一般的 TV-Hilbert 模型。为了说明学习算法的潜力，我们班级中的新颖（非学习）选择在去噪和纹理去除方面显示了可比较的结果。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一类广义交集算法并研究其不动点性质，用于改进图像分解学习的理论基础。
AI.motivation: 传统图像分解方法依赖经验设计的函数优化，缺乏理论保证；而学习型算法虽效果好但理论支撑不足，需建立统一框架证明其收敛性。
AI.method: 形式化定义包含多种图像分解模型的广义交点问题，证明其子类算法存在不动点，并推广经典TV-l2等变分模型。
AI.result: 理论证明了算法不动点的存在性，新提出的非学习方法在去噪和纹理移除任务中表现与现有方法相当。
AI.conclusion: 通过理论分析与实验验证，为学习型图像分解算法提供了理论保障，并展示了广义框架的有效性。

title: Semantics of the Black-Box: Can knowledge graphs help make deep learning systems more interpretable and explainable?
title_zh: 黑盒语义：知识图谱能否帮助深度学习系统变得更加可解释和解释？
comment: 6 pages + references, 4 figures, Accepted to IEEE internet computing 2020
comments: 6 pages + references, 4 figures, Accepted to IEEE internet computing 2020
abstract: The recent series of innovations in deep learning (DL) have shown enormous potential to impact individuals and society, both positively and negatively. The DL models utilizing massive computing power and enormous datasets have significantly outperformed prior historical benchmarks on increasingly difficult, well-defined research tasks across technology domains such as computer vision, natural language processing, signal processing, and human-computer interactions. However, the Black-Box nature of DL models and their over-reliance on massive amounts of data condensed into labels and dense representations poses challenges for interpretability and explainability of the system. Furthermore, DLs have not yet been proven in their ability to effectively utilize relevant domain knowledge and experience critical to human understanding. This aspect is missing in early data-focused approaches and necessitated knowledge-infused learning and other strategies to incorporate computational knowledge. This article demonstrates how knowledge, provided as a knowledge graph, is incorporated into DL methods using knowledge-infused learning, which is one of the strategies. We then discuss how this makes a fundamental difference in the interpretability and explainability of current approaches, and illustrate it with examples from natural language processing for healthcare and education applications.
summary: The recent series of innovations in deep learning (DL) have shown enormous potential to impact individuals and society, both positively and negatively. The DL models utilizing massive computing power and enormous datasets have significantly outperformed prior historical benchmarks on increasingly difficult, well-defined research tasks across technology domains such as computer vision, natural language processing, signal processing, and human-computer interactions. However, the Black-Box nature of DL models and their over-reliance on massive amounts of data condensed into labels and dense representations poses challenges for interpretability and explainability of the system. Furthermore, DLs have not yet been proven in their ability to effectively utilize relevant domain knowledge and experience critical to human understanding. This aspect is missing in early data-focused approaches and necessitated knowledge-infused learning and other strategies to incorporate computational knowledge. This article demonstrates how knowledge, provided as a knowledge graph, is incorporated into DL methods using knowledge-infused learning, which is one of the strategies. We then discuss how this makes a fundamental difference in the interpretability and explainability of current approaches, and illustrate it with examples from natural language processing for healthcare and education applications.
abstract_zh: 最近深度学习 (DL) 领域的一系列创新显示出对个人和社会产生积极和消极影响的巨大潜力。在计算机视觉、自然语言处理、信号处理和人机交互等技术领域日益困难、定义明确的研究任务上，利用大量计算能力和庞大数据集的深度学习模型显着优于以前的历史基准。然而，深度学习模型的黑盒性质及其对压缩为标签和密集表示的大量数据的过度依赖，给系统的可解释性和可解释性带来了挑战。此外，深度学习有效利用对人类理解至关重要的相关领域知识和经验的能力尚未得到证实。早期以数据为中心的方法缺少这一方面，因此需要注入知识的学习和其他策略来整合计算知识。本文演示了如何使用知识注入学习（其中一种策略）将以知识图形式提供的知识纳入深度学习方法中。然后，我们讨论这如何对当前方法的可解释性和可解释性产生根本性的影响，并通过医疗保健和教育应用的自然语言处理示例进行说明。

AI.tldr: 探讨知识图谱如何通过知识注入学习提升深度学习系统的可解释性与可解释性
AI.motivation: 深度学习模型虽性能强大但存在黑箱特性，缺乏对领域知识的有效利用，导致可解释性不足
AI.method: 采用知识注入学习策略，将知识图谱结构化知识整合到深度学习模型中
AI.result: 验证了知识注入学习在提升模型可解释性方面的有效性，并通过医疗和教育领域的NLP实例进行了验证
AI.conclusion: 结合知识图谱的深度学习方法能有效提升系统的可解释性与透明度，为构建更可信的AI系统提供新思路

search_text: Semantics of the Black-Box: Can knowledge graphs help make deep learning systems more interpretable and explainable? 黑盒语义：知识图谱能否帮助深度学习系统变得更加可解释和解释？ The recent series of innovations in deep learning (DL) have shown enormous potential to impact individuals and society, both positively and negatively. The DL models utilizing massive computing power and enormous datasets have significantly outperformed prior historical benchmarks on increasingly difficult, well-defined research tasks across technology domains such as computer vision, natural language processing, signal processing, and human-computer interactions. However, the Black-Box nature of DL models and their over-reliance on massive amounts of data condensed into labels and dense representations poses challenges for interpretability and explainability of the system. Furthermore, DLs have not yet been proven in their ability to effectively utilize relevant domain knowledge and experience critical to human understanding. This aspect is missing in early data-focused approaches and necessitated knowledge-infused learning and other strategies to incorporate computational knowledge. This article demonstrates how knowledge, provided as a knowledge graph, is incorporated into DL methods using knowledge-infused learning, which is one of the strategies. We then discuss how this makes a fundamental difference in the interpretability and explainability of current approaches, and illustrate it with examples from natural language processing for healthcare and education applications. 最近深度学习 (DL) 领域的一系列创新显示出对个人和社会产生积极和消极影响的巨大潜力。在计算机视觉、自然语言处理、信号处理和人机交互等技术领域日益困难、定义明确的研究任务上，利用大量计算能力和庞大数据集的深度学习模型显着优于以前的历史基准。然而，深度学习模型的黑盒性质及其对压缩为标签和密集表示的大量数据的过度依赖，给系统的可解释性和可解释性带来了挑战。此外，深度学习有效利用对人类理解至关重要的相关领域知识和经验的能力尚未得到证实。早期以数据为中心的方法缺少这一方面，因此需要注入知识的学习和其他策略来整合计算知识。本文演示了如何使用知识注入学习（其中一种策略）将以知识图形式提供的知识纳入深度学习方法中。然后，我们讨论这如何对当前方法的可解释性和可解释性产生根本性的影响，并通过医疗保健和教育应用的自然语言处理示例进行说明。 探讨知识图谱如何通过知识注入学习提升深度学习系统的可解释性与可解释性 Manas Gaur,Keyur Faldu,Amit Sheth cs.AI

authors_text: Federico Pernici,Matteo Bruni,Claudio Baecchi,Francesco Turchini,Alberto Del Bimbo
title: Class-incremental Learning with Pre-allocated Fixed Classifiers
title_zh: 使用预先分配的固定分类器进行班级增量学习
comment: ICPR 2021 (figure and typos fixed)
comments: ICPR 2021 (figure and typos fixed)
abstract: In class-incremental learning, a learning agent faces a stream of data with the goal of learning new classes while not forgetting previous ones. Neural networks are known to suffer under this setting, as they forget previously acquired knowledge. To address this problem, effective methods exploit past data stored in an episodic memory while expanding the final classifier nodes to accommodate the new classes. In this work, we substitute the expanding classifier with a novel fixed classifier in which a number of pre-allocated output nodes are subject to the classification loss right from the beginning of the learning phase. Contrarily to the standard expanding classifier, this allows: (a) the output nodes of future unseen classes to firstly see negative samples since the beginning of learning together with the positive samples that incrementally arrive; (b) to learn features that do not change their geometric configuration as novel classes are incorporated in the learning model. Experiments with public datasets show that the proposed approach is as effective as the expanding classifier while exhibiting novel intriguing properties of the internal feature representation that are otherwise not-existent. Our ablation study on pre-allocating a large number of classes further validates the approach.
summary: In class-incremental learning, a learning agent faces a stream of data with the goal of learning new classes while not forgetting previous ones. Neural networks are known to suffer under this setting, as they forget previously acquired knowledge. To address this problem, effective methods exploit past data stored in an episodic memory while expanding the final classifier nodes to accommodate the new classes. In this work, we substitute the expanding classifier with a novel fixed classifier in which a number of pre-allocated output nodes are subject to the classification loss right from the beginning of the learning phase. Contrarily to the standard expanding classifier, this allows: (a) the output nodes of future unseen classes to firstly see negative samples since the beginning of learning together with the positive samples that incrementally arrive; (b) to learn features that do not change their geometric configuration as novel classes are incorporated in the learning model. Experiments with public datasets show that the proposed approach is as effective as the expanding classifier while exhibiting novel intriguing properties of the internal feature representation that are otherwise not-existent. Our ablation study on pre-allocating a large number of classes further validates the approach.
abstract_zh: 在类增量学习中，学习代理面对数据流，其目标是学习新类，同时不忘记以前的类。众所周知，神经网络在这种情况下会受到影响，因为它们会忘记以前获得的知识。为了解决这个问题，有效的方法利用存储在情景存储器中的过去数据，同时扩展最终分类器节点以适应新的类别。在这项工作中，我们用一种新颖的固定分类器代替扩展分类器，其中许多预先分配的输出节点从学习阶段开始就受到分类损失的影响。与标准扩展分类器相反，这允许：（a）未来未见过的类的输出节点首先看到自学习开始以来的负样本以及逐渐到达的正样本； （b）学习在学习模型中纳入新类时不会改变其几何配置的特征。对公共数据集的实验表明，所提出的方法与扩展分类器一样有效，同时展示了内部特征表示的新颖有趣的特性，而这些特性在其他情况下是不存在的。我们对预分配大量类的消融研究进一步验证了该方法。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种新的类增量学习方法，通过预分配固定分类器替代传统扩展分类器，解决神经网络在增量学习中的遗忘问题。
AI.motivation: 传统扩展分类器在增量学习中需不断扩展节点，导致特征表示随新类加入而变化，可能遗忘旧知识。预分配固定分类器可使未来类别节点从学习初期接触负样本，保持特征几何结构稳定。
AI.method: 采用预分配固定分类器，从学习开始即为未来类别节点分配固定输出节点，使其同时接收负样本和增量正样本，学习不随新类加入而改变的特征表示。
AI.result: 实验表明该方法与扩展分类器效果相当，但具有独特的特征表示性质，消融实验证实预分配大量类别可进一步提升性能。
AI.conclusion: 预分配固定分类器在类增量学习中有效，且能保持特征表示稳定性，为增量学习提供了新思路。

title: Cross-Lingual Relation Extraction with Transformers
title_zh: 使用 Transformers 进行跨语言关系提取
comment: 11 pages
comments: 11 pages
abstract: Relation extraction (RE) is one of the most important tasks in information extraction, as it provides essential information for many NLP applications. In this paper, we propose a cross-lingual RE approach that does not require any human annotation in a target language or any cross-lingual resources. Building upon unsupervised cross-lingual representation learning frameworks, we develop several deep Transformer based RE models with a novel encoding scheme that can effectively encode both entity location and entity type information. Our RE models, when trained with English data, outperform several deep neural network based English RE models. More importantly, our models can be applied to perform zero-shot cross-lingual RE, achieving the state-of-the-art cross-lingual RE performance on two datasets (68-89% of the accuracy of the supervised target-language RE model). The high cross-lingual transfer efficiency without requiring additional training data or cross-lingual resources shows that our RE models are especially useful for low-resource languages.
summary: Relation extraction (RE) is one of the most important tasks in information extraction, as it provides essential information for many NLP applications. In this paper, we propose a cross-lingual RE approach that does not require any human annotation in a target language or any cross-lingual resources. Building upon unsupervised cross-lingual representation learning frameworks, we develop several deep Transformer based RE models with a novel encoding scheme that can effectively encode both entity location and entity type information. Our RE models, when trained with English data, outperform several deep neural network based English RE models. More importantly, our models can be applied to perform zero-shot cross-lingual RE, achieving the state-of-the-art cross-lingual RE performance on two datasets (68-89% of the accuracy of the supervised target-language RE model). The high cross-lingual transfer efficiency without requiring additional training data or cross-lingual resources shows that our RE models are especially useful for low-resource languages.
abstract_zh: 关系提取（RE）是信息提取中最重要的任务之一，因为它为许多 NLP 应用提供了必要的信息。在本文中，我们提出了一种跨语言 RE 方法，不需要目标语言或任何跨语言资源的任何人工注释。基于无监督的跨语言表示学习框架，我们开发了几种基于 Transformer 的深度 RE 模型，其新颖的编码方案可以有效地编码实体位置和实体类型信息。当使用英语数据进行训练时，我们的 RE 模型优于几种基于深度神经网络的英语 RE 模型。更重要的是，我们的模型可以应用于执行零样本跨语言 RE，在两个数据集上实现最先进的跨语言 RE 性能（监督目标语言 RE 模型的准确度为 68-89%）。无需额外的训练数据或跨语言资源即可实现高跨语言传输效率，这表明我们的 RE 模型对于资源匮乏的语言特别有用。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种无需目标语言标注或跨语言资源的跨语言关系抽取方法，基于Transformer模型和无监督跨语言表示学习框架。
AI.motivation: 传统关系抽取方法依赖目标语言标注数据或跨语言资源，而低资源语言缺乏这些条件，需开发无需额外数据的跨语言模型。
AI.method: 构建基于Transformer的深度模型，采用新型编码方案同时编码实体位置和类型信息，并利用无监督跨语言表示学习框架进行训练。
AI.result: 英语训练模型在英语数据集上超越现有深度模型，零样本跨语言关系抽取在两个数据集上达到监督模型68-89%的准确率。
AI.conclusion: 该方法无需额外训练数据或跨语言资源即可实现高效跨语言迁移，特别适用于低资源语言的关系抽取任务。

tldr: 本文提出了一种无需目标语言标注或跨语言资源的跨语言关系抽取方法，基于Transformer模型和无监督跨语言表示学习框架。
motivation: 传统关系抽取方法依赖目标语言标注数据或跨语言资源，而低资源语言缺乏这些条件，需开发无需额外数据的跨语言模型。
method: 构建基于Transformer的深度模型，采用新型编码方案同时编码实体位置和类型信息，并利用无监督跨语言表示学习框架进行训练。
result: 英语训练模型在英语数据集上超越现有深度模型，零样本跨语言关系抽取在两个数据集上达到监督模型68-89%的准确率。
conclusion: 该方法无需额外训练数据或跨语言资源即可实现高效跨语言迁移，特别适用于低资源语言的关系抽取任务。
search_text: Cross-Lingual Relation Extraction with Transformers 使用 Transformers 进行跨语言关系提取 Relation extraction (RE) is one of the most important tasks in information extraction, as it provides essential information for many NLP applications. In this paper, we propose a cross-lingual RE approach that does not require any human annotation in a target language or any cross-lingual resources. Building upon unsupervised cross-lingual representation learning frameworks, we develop several deep Transformer based RE models with a novel encoding scheme that can effectively encode both entity location and entity type information. Our RE models, when trained with English data, outperform several deep neural network based English RE models. More importantly, our models can be applied to perform zero-shot cross-lingual RE, achieving the state-of-the-art cross-lingual RE performance on two datasets (68-89% of the accuracy of the supervised target-language RE model). The high cross-lingual transfer efficiency without requiring additional training data or cross-lingual resources shows that our RE models are especially useful for low-resource languages. 关系提取（RE）是信息提取中最重要的任务之一，因为它为许多 NLP 应用提供了必要的信息。在本文中，我们提出了一种跨语言 RE 方法，不需要目标语言或任何跨语言资源的任何人工注释。基于无监督的跨语言表示学习框架，我们开发了几种基于 Transformer 的深度 RE 模型，其新颖的编码方案可以有效地编码实体位置和实体类型信息。当使用英语数据进行训练时，我们的 RE 模型优于几种基于深度神经网络的英语 RE 模型。更重要的是，我们的模型可以应用于执行零样本跨语言 RE，在两个数据集上实现最先进的跨语言 RE 性能（监督目标语言 RE 模型的准确度为 68-89%）。无需额外的训练数据或跨语言资源即可实现高跨语言传输效率，这表明我们的 RE 模型对于资源匮乏的语言特别有用。 本文提出了一种无需目标语言标注或跨语言资源的跨语言关系抽取方法，基于Transformer模型和无监督跨语言表示学习框架。 Jian Ni,Taesun Moon,Parul Awasthy,Radu Florian cs.CL,cs.AI,cs.IR,cs.LG

authors_text: Tianyu Ma,Hang Zhang,Hanley Ong,Amar Vora,Thanh D. Nguyen,Ajay Gupta,Yi Wang,Mert Sabuncu
title: Ensembling Low Precision Models for Binary Biomedical Image Segmentation
title_zh: 用于二元生物医学图像分割的低精度模型集成
comment: 10 pages, 4 figures
comments: 10 pages, 4 figures
abstract: Segmentation of anatomical regions of interest such as vessels or small lesions in medical images is still a difficult problem that is often tackled with manual input by an expert. One of the major challenges for this task is that the appearance of foreground (positive) regions can be similar to background (negative) regions. As a result, many automatic segmentation algorithms tend to exhibit asymmetric errors, typically producing more false positives than false negatives. In this paper, we aim to leverage this asymmetry and train a diverse ensemble of models with very high recall, while sacrificing their precision. Our core idea is straightforward: A diverse ensemble of low precision and high recall models are likely to make different false positive errors (classifying background as foreground in different parts of the image), but the true positives will tend to be consistent. Thus, in aggregate the false positive errors will cancel out, yielding high performance for the ensemble. Our strategy is general and can be applied with any segmentation model. In three different applications (carotid artery segmentation in a neck CT angiography, myocardium segmentation in a cardiovascular MRI and multiple sclerosis lesion segmentation in a brain MRI), we show how the proposed approach can significantly boost the performance of a baseline segmentation method.
summary: Segmentation of anatomical regions of interest such as vessels or small lesions in medical images is still a difficult problem that is often tackled with manual input by an expert. One of the major challenges for this task is that the appearance of foreground (positive) regions can be similar to background (negative) regions. As a result, many automatic segmentation algorithms tend to exhibit asymmetric errors, typically producing more false positives than false negatives. In this paper, we aim to leverage this asymmetry and train a diverse ensemble of models with very high recall, while sacrificing their precision. Our core idea is straightforward: A diverse ensemble of low precision and high recall models are likely to make different false positive errors (classifying background as foreground in different parts of the image), but the true positives will tend to be consistent. Thus, in aggregate the false positive errors will cancel out, yielding high performance for the ensemble. Our strategy is general and can be applied with any segmentation model. In three different applications (carotid artery segmentation in a neck CT angiography, myocardium segmentation in a cardiovascular MRI and multiple sclerosis lesion segmentation in a brain MRI), we show how the proposed approach can significantly boost the performance of a baseline segmentation method.
abstract_zh: 医学图像中感兴趣的解剖区域（例如血管或小病变）的分割仍然是一个难题，通常由专家手动输入来解决。这项任务的主要挑战之一是前景（正）区域的外观可能与背景（负）区域相似。因此，许多自动分割算法往往会出现不对称错误，通常产生的误报多于漏报。在本文中，我们的目标是利用这种不对称性，训练具有极高召回率的多样化模型集合，同时牺牲其精度。我们的核心思想很简单：低精度和高召回率模型的多样化组合可能会产生不同的误报错误（将图像不同部分的背景分类为前景），但真正的误报往往是一致的。因此，总的来说，误报错误将被抵消，从而为整体带来高性能。我们的策略是通用的，可以应用于任何细分模型。在三种不同的应用中（颈部 CT 血管造影中的颈动脉分割、心血管 MRI 中的心肌分割和脑 MRI 中的多发性硬化症病变分割），我们展示了所提出的方法如何显着提高基线分割方法的性能。

AI.tldr: 本文旨在通过集成多个低精度但高召回率的模型，提高二值生物医学图像分割的性能。
AI.motivation: 医学图像中解剖区域的分割具有挑战性，尤其因为前景和背景的外观相似，导致自动分割算法容易出现较多的假阳性错误。
AI.method: 本文提出了一种通用策略，即训练一个由多个低精度但高召回率模型组成的多样化集成，利用这些模型在不同区域产生不同的假阳性错误，而真正阳性结果则较为一致，从而在整体上抵消假阳性错误。
AI.result: 在三种不同的医学图像分割任务中（颈动脉CT血管造影、心血管MRI心肌分割和脑MRI多发性硬化症病灶分割），该方法显著提升了基线分割方法的性能。
AI.conclusion: 该方法通过集成多个低精度模型，有效提高了二值生物医学图像分割的准确性，具有广泛的适用性。

authors_text: Xiangwei Shi,Seyran Khademi,Yunqiang Li,Jan van Gemert
title: Zoom-CAM: Generating Fine-grained Pixel Annotations from Image Labels
title_zh: Zoom-CAM：从图像标签生成细粒度像素注释
comment: ICPR 2020
comments: ICPR 2020
abstract: Current weakly supervised object localization and segmentation rely on class-discriminative visualization techniques to generate pseudo-labels for pixel-level training. Such visualization methods, including class activation mapping (CAM) and Grad-CAM, use only the deepest, lowest resolution convolutional layer, missing all information in intermediate layers. We propose Zoom-CAM: going beyond the last lowest resolution layer by integrating the importance maps over all activations in intermediate layers. Zoom-CAM captures fine-grained small-scale objects for various discriminative class instances, which are commonly missed by the baseline visualization methods. We focus on generating pixel-level pseudo-labels from class labels. The quality of our pseudo-labels evaluated on the ImageNet localization task exhibits more than 2.8% improvement on top-1 error. For weakly supervised semantic segmentation our generated pseudo-labels improve a state of the art model by 1.1%.
summary: Current weakly supervised object localization and segmentation rely on class-discriminative visualization techniques to generate pseudo-labels for pixel-level training. Such visualization methods, including class activation mapping (CAM) and Grad-CAM, use only the deepest, lowest resolution convolutional layer, missing all information in intermediate layers. We propose Zoom-CAM: going beyond the last lowest resolution layer by integrating the importance maps over all activations in intermediate layers. Zoom-CAM captures fine-grained small-scale objects for various discriminative class instances, which are commonly missed by the baseline visualization methods. We focus on generating pixel-level pseudo-labels from class labels. The quality of our pseudo-labels evaluated on the ImageNet localization task exhibits more than 2.8% improvement on top-1 error. For weakly supervised semantic segmentation our generated pseudo-labels improve a state of the art model by 1.1%.
abstract_zh: 当前的弱监督对象定位和分割依赖于类判别可视化技术来生成用于像素级训练的伪标签。此类可视化方法，包括类激活映射 (CAM) 和 Grad-CAM，仅使用最深、分辨率最低的卷积层，忽略中间层中的所有信息。我们提出 Zoom-CAM：通过集成中间层中所有激活的重要性图来超越最后一个最低分辨率层。 Zoom-CAM 为各种有区别的类实例捕获细粒度的小规模对象，而基线可视化方法通常会忽略这些对象。我们专注于从类标签生成像素级伪标签。在 ImageNet 本地化任务上评估的伪标签的质量比 top-1 错误提高了 2.8% 以上。对于弱监督语义分割，我们生成的伪标签将最先进的模型提高了 1.1%。

AI.tldr: 本文提出Zoom-CAM方法，通过整合中间层特征生成更精确的像素级伪标签，提升弱监督目标定位与分割性能。
AI.motivation: 现有CAM类方法仅依赖最后一层低分辨率特征，导致细粒度小物体定位不准确且信息丢失。
AI.method: 设计Zoom-CAM算法，聚合所有中间层激活特征的重要性图，捕捉多尺度判别信息生成像素级伪标注。
AI.result: ImageNet定位任务top-1错误率降低2.8%，弱监督分割任务较SOTA模型提升1.1%性能指标。
AI.conclusion: 通过多层特征融合有效提升伪标签质量，为弱监督视觉任务提供更精细的像素级标注方案。

ai_content.tldr: 本文提出Zoom-CAM方法，通过整合中间层特征生成更精确的像素级伪标签，提升弱监督目标定位与分割性能。

title: A general approach to compute the relevance of middle-level input features
title_zh: 计算中级输入特征相关性的通用方法
comment: Presented on the Explainable Deep Learning/AI (EDL/AI) Workshop during the 25th International Conference on Pattern Recognition (ICPR2020)
comments: Presented on the Explainable Deep Learning/AI (EDL/AI) Workshop during the 25th International Conference on Pattern Recognition (ICPR2020)
abstract: This work proposes a novel general framework, in the context of eXplainable Artificial Intelligence (XAI), to construct explanations for the behaviour of Machine Learning (ML) models in terms of middle-level features. One can isolate two different ways to provide explanations in the context of XAI: low and middle-level explanations. Middle-level explanations have been introduced for alleviating some deficiencies of low-level explanations such as, in the context of image classification, the fact that human users are left with a significant interpretive burden: starting from low-level explanations, one has to identify properties of the overall input that are perceptually salient for the human visual system. However, a general approach to correctly evaluate the elements of middle-level explanations with respect ML model responses has never been proposed in the literature.
summary: This work proposes a novel general framework, in the context of eXplainable Artificial Intelligence (XAI), to construct explanations for the behaviour of Machine Learning (ML) models in terms of middle-level features. One can isolate two different ways to provide explanations in the context of XAI: low and middle-level explanations. Middle-level explanations have been introduced for alleviating some deficiencies of low-level explanations such as, in the context of image classification, the fact that human users are left with a significant interpretive burden: starting from low-level explanations, one has to identify properties of the overall input that are perceptually salient for the human visual system. However, a general approach to correctly evaluate the elements of middle-level explanations with respect ML model responses has never been proposed in the literature.
abstract_zh: 这项工作在可解释人工智能（XAI）的背景下提出了一种新颖的通用框架，以根据中层特征构建机器学习（ML）模型行为的解释。在 XAI 的背景下，我们可以分离出两种不同的方式来提供解释：低级解释和中级解释。中级解释的引入是为了减轻低级解释的一些缺陷，例如在图像分类的背景下，人类用户面临着巨大的解释负担：从低级解释开始，人们必须识别对人类视觉系统感知显着的整体输入的属性。然而，文献中从未提出过一种正确评估 ML 模型响应的中层解释要素的通用方法。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种通用框架，用于在可解释人工智能（XAI）中通过中层特征解释机器学习模型的行为。
AI.motivation: 现有低层解释方法在图像分类等任务中需用户自行识别感知显著特征，导致解释负担过重，而中层解释的评估方法在文献中尚未被系统提出。
AI.method: 构建一个通用框架，通过中层特征量化模型响应的相关性，弥补低层解释的不足。
AI.result: 首次提出针对中层解释的通用评估方法，为XAI领域提供了新的理论工具。
AI.conclusion: 该框架有效解决了中层解释评估的空白，为模型行为解释提供了更符合人类认知的途径。
